sections.paragraph:
διασπαράσσω 1 aor. διεσπάραξα; pass. ptc. n. διασπαραχθείς (Just.) (s. σπαράσσω; Aeschyl. et al.; Parthenius 10, 3 [of dogs]; TestSol 4:1 D; JosAs 12:10 cod. A; Just., A I, 21, 2 al. Διόνυσον) tear in pieces of wolves (Aesop, Fab. 165 H.= Syntipas 6, 2f [H-H.]: the wolf tears the sheep) τὰ ἀρνία 2 Cl 5:3.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Parthenius Parthenius , I b.c.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

2 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 236
